name: Wirehaired Pointing Griffon
description: The Wirehaired Pointing Griffon is often called the supreme gundog due to its versatile hunting ability on both land and water. Bred by Eduard Korthals in the 19th century, the breed combines pointing and retrieving skills with a rugged, weather-resistant coat. They are devoted family companions who thrive with active owners and ample outdoor exercise.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Elbow Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Elbow dysplasia is an umbrella term covering several developmental abnormalities of the elbow joint including fragmented coronoid process, osteochondritis dissecans, and ununited anconeal process. It causes foreleg lameness and arthritis.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Surgical intervention is often recommended to remove bone fragments or reshape joint surfaces. Medical management includes NSAIDs, joint supplements, and physiotherapy.
prevention_tips: Control growth rate in large breeds

conditions_detail.name: Otitis Externa (Ear Infections)
conditions_detail.description: Otitis externa is inflammation of the external ear canal, usually caused by bacterial or yeast (Malassezia) overgrowth secondary to underlying factors such as allergies, foreign bodies, or conformational abnormalities. It is the most common reason for veterinary visits.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment involves thorough ear cleaning and topical ear drops containing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory agents. Chronic or recurrent cases require investigation of underlying causes such as allergies.
prevention_tips: Regular ear inspection and gentle cleaning
Dry ears thoroughly after swimming or bathing
Treat allergies to prevent recurrent infections
Regular grooming of ear hair in predisposed breeds

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings
